perturbation.original_text: Each of Photronics and DNP is hereinafter referred to as a "Shareholder" and collectively as the "Shareholders", each of the Shareholders and PDMC is hereinafter referred to as a "Supplier" and collectively as the "Suppliers", and each of the Suppliers and the Company is hereinafter referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."
perturbation.changed_text: Each of Photronics and DNP is hereinafter referred to as a "Shareholder" and collectively as the "Shareholders", each of the Shareholders and PDMC is hereinafter referred to as a "Vendor" and collectively as the "Vendors", and each of the Vendors and the Company is hereinafter referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."
perturbation.explanation: The contract initially defines 'Supplier' to include 'Shareholders and PDMC'. By changing 'Supplier' to 'Vendor,' the contract now implies 'Vendor' includes 'Shareholders and PDMC.' This creates a potential in-text contradiction because later sections reference obligations of the 'Suppliers,' creating ambiguity if 'Vendors' and 'Suppliers' are interchangable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Suppliers will make good faith efforts to accept all [***]from the Company that comply with this Agreement including adhering to all relevant specifications of the Product as set forth in the [***]entered into between the Company and the Supplier (including the [***] (as defined below)). The Suppliers shall notify the Company of acceptance or rejection of a [***]within [***]hours of receipt of a [***]. Failure of the Suppliers to accept or reject a [***]within [***]hours shall constitute acceptance of such [***]. The lead time for the Products will be as set forth in the applicable [***]. Each [***]shall include the following: (a) the Company's [***]number; (b) identification of the quantity and type of the Product ordered by the Company; (c) the price of each Product ordered per Schedule 2 attached hereto; (d) the requested delivery date (subject to the applicable Product Lead Time); (e) any shipping instructions, including preferred carrier and shipping destination; and (f) the specifications for the Product. Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement and the China JV Operating Agreement to the contrary, and for the sake of clarity, [***].
perturbation.justification: The modification redefines 'Suppliers' as 'Vendors'. The contradicted text explicitly refers to obligations and actions of 'The Suppliers', such as accepting orders and providing lead times. By changing 'Supplier' to 'Vendor' in the initial definition, the contract creates ambiguity as to whether 'Vendors' also have these obligations, thus potentially creating a contradiction and uncertainty in enforcement. The term Supplier is no longer clearly defined, thus creating a misaligned terminology contradiction as defined above.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 Outsource and Issuance of Purchase Orders Subject to the terms and conditions mentioned hereunder, the Parties agree to the outsource model based on [***]as follows, and the Parties also agree that they may add additional Products to this Agreement through additional Purchase Orders [***] For the avoidance of doubt, the outsource model is purely made based on the [***] Moreover, it is acknowledged by the Parties that[***] Therefore, subject to the prior notification to, and the instruction and the express approval of the customers, the Steering Committee could reasonably decide or change the outsource model at its own discretion in accordance with the China JV Operating Agreement. In any case, none of the Parties shall unreasonably [***] of the Products to take advantage of the outsource relationship or [***]
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 Outsource and Issuance of Purchase Orders Subject to the terms and conditions mentioned hereunder, the Parties agree to the outsource model based on [***]as follows, and the Parties also agree that they may add additional Deliverables to this Agreement through additional Purchase Orders [***] For the avoidance of doubt, the outsource model is purely made based on the [***] Moreover, it is acknowledged by the Parties that[***] Therefore, subject to the prior notification to, and the instruction and the express approval of the customers, the Steering Committee could reasonably decide or change the outsource model at its own discretion in accordance with the China JV Operating Agreement. In any case, none of the Parties shall unreasonably [***] of the Deliverables to take advantage of the outsource relationship or [***]
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Products' to 'Deliverables' within the context of 'Purchase Orders', it creates confusion about what a Purchase Order covers. The document defines 'Product' specifically, but now 'Deliverables' is introduced without definition, potentially leading to disputes over what can be added to the agreement via purchase orders. The document later specifies in section 2.2 Product's specifics about a purchase order, thus if Deliverables and Product are interchangable as the contract suggests with this change, there could be uncertainty of the terms or specifications mentioned later in 2.2
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Suppliers will make good faith efforts to accept all [***]from the Company that comply with this Agreement including adhering to all relevant specifications of the Product as set forth in the [***]entered into between the Company and the Supplier (including the [***] (as defined below)). The Suppliers shall notify the Company of acceptance or rejection of a [***]within [***]hours of receipt of a [***]. Failure of the Suppliers to accept or reject a [***]within [***]hours shall constitute acceptance of such [***]. The lead time for the Products will be as set forth in the applicable [***]. Each [***]shall include the following: (a) the Company's [***]number; (b) identification of the quantity and type of the Product ordered by the Company; (c) the price of each Product ordered per Schedule 2 attached hereto; (d) the requested delivery date (subject to the applicable Product Lead Time); (e) any shipping instructions, including preferred carrier and shipping destination; and (f) the specifications for the Product. Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement and the China JV Operating Agreement to the contrary, and for the sake of clarity, [***].
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Products' to 'Deliverables' introduces an undefined term ('Deliverables') that is used interchangeably with the defined term 'Products' in the context of Purchase Orders. Section 2.2 (Contradicted Text) specifies the requirements for purchase orders, including adhering to 'all relevant specifications of the Product'. If 'Deliverables' are now also added through purchase orders (as implied by the change in Section 2.1), and 'Deliverables' are not defined, it creates ambiguity and contradiction. It is unclear whether the same specifications mentioned in 2.2 apply to 'Deliverables'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it makes the contract uncertain and potentially unenforceable. The misaligned terminology directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Invoices; Payments The Suppliers shall issue invoices to the Company for any amounts payable to the Suppliers pursuant to this Agreement upon shipment of the applicable Products to the Company. Payments for Products delivered in accordance with the Purchase Orders, and any other to be made by the Company to Suppliers hereunder, shall be made in the Applicable Currency within [***]from the shipment of the applicable Products delivered.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Invoices; Payments The Suppliers shall issue invoices to the Company for any amounts payable to the Suppliers pursuant to this Agreement upon shipment of the applicable Goods to the Company. Payments for Products delivered in accordance with the Purchase Orders, and any other to be made by the Company to Suppliers hereunder, shall be made in the Applicable Currency within [***]from the shipment of the applicable Goods delivered.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Products' to 'Goods' in this section creates a contradiction because 'Product' is explicitly defined in Schedule 1, while 'Goods' is not. The original term 'Products' specifically refers to 'photolithographic integrated circuit photomasks', this creates an in-text contradiction in Article 6 with Limited Warranties, because this article is specified towards Products and not Goods.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the Suppliers warrants that the Products shall comply with the specifications and documentation agreed by the relevant Supplier and the Company in writing that is applicable to such Products for the Warranty Period. This warranty does not apply to any Product failures resulting from misuse, storage in or exposure to environmental conditions inconsistent with those specified in the applicable specifications or documentation, modification of the Product by anyone other than the relevant Supplier. If a Product fails to comply with the foregoing warranty, the relevant Supplier shall, at its option, either [***]such Product, or, in the event the foregoing options are not commercially practicable, [***]to the Company any amounts paid for the applicable Product. Without limiting the remedies specified in Article 8 and Section 9.2, this Section 6.1 states the exclusive remedy of the Company for failure of a Product to conform to the warranty provisions set forth in this Section 6.1.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Products' to 'Goods' introduces a contradiction. 'Products' is explicitly defined in Schedule 1, while 'Goods' is not. The contradicted text specifies warranties applicable to 'Products' which, according to the explanation, means 'photolithographic integrated circuit photomasks'. By changing the term to 'Goods', the warranty's applicability becomes unclear, leading to a strong contradiction because 'Goods' is undefined and potentially broader, thus creating uncertainty in the warranty obligations, which aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction. The explanation states 'this creates an in-text contradiction in Article 6 with Limited Warranties, because this article is specified towards Products and not Goods'.
